from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count
import pytest

def test_none_and_empty_input():
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0

def test_count_senders_case_insensitive_sender_key():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user"},
        {"sender": "TENANT"},
        {"sender": "Outbound"},
        {"sender": "ai"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "other"},
    ]
    # Only first 4 count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_count_senders_fallback_to_direction_key():
    messages = [
        {"direction": "user"},
        {"direction": "TENANT"},
        {"direction": "Outbound"},
        {"direction": "ai"},
        {"direction": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"direction": "other"},
    ]
    # Only first 4 count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_sender_key_precedence_over_direction():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "direction": "user"},  # sender key takes precedence, landlord ignored
        {"sender": "user", "direction": "landlord"},  # sender key user counts
        {"sender": "inbound", "direction": "tenant"}, # sender key inbound ignored
        {"sender": "outbound", "direction": "inbound"}, # sender key outbound counts
    ]
    # Only messages with sender keys "user" and "outbound" count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 2

def test_case_insensitivity_and_partial_words_not_counted():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User"},       # counts
        {"sender": "tenant"},     # counts
        {"sender": "OUTBOUND"},   # counts
        {"sender": "Ai"},         # counts
        {"sender": "tenant-agent"}, # does NOT count, partial word
        {"sender": "user123"},    # does NOT count
        {"sender": "landlord"},   # does NOT count
        {"direction": "Inbound"}, # does NOT count
    ]
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_mixed_valid_and_invalid_messages():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user"},
        {"direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "ai"},
        {"direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "unknown"},
        {"direction": "unknown"},
    ]
    # Count user, tenant, ai, outbound only once each
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 5

from app.ai.stages import detect_stage
from app.db.status import VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_empty_and_none_input_returns_none():
    assert detect_stage([]) is None
    assert detect_stage(None) is None


def test_only_most_recent_8_messages_considered():
    # 9 messages, first contains booking confirmation and time, but should be ignored
    messages = [
        {"message": "Confirmed, see you Thursday at 2pm", "sender": "landlord"},
    ] + [{"message": "Random old message", "sender": "tenant"}] * 8
    # The last 8 messages have no booking confirmation or time, so result is None
    assert detect_stage(messages) is None

    # Now put the booking confirmation and time inside the last 8 messages
    messages = [{"message": "Random old message", "sender": "tenant"}] + [
        {"message": "Confirmed, see you Thursday at 2pm", "sender": "landlord"},
    ] + [{"message": "Random message", "sender": "tenant"}] * 6
    # The last 8 messages include the booking confirmation and time, so VIEWING_BOOKED
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_viewing_discussion_for_negation_keywords_mid_sentence_case_insensitive():
    messages = [
        {"message": "I think we need to reschedule the viewing", "sender": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

    messages = [
        {"message": "Can we rearrange the appointment?", "sender": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

    messages = [
        {"message": "Let's do it another time", "sender": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

    messages = [
        {"message": "I need to change the date", "sender": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

    messages = [
        {"message": "Instead, can we meet next week?", "sender": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION


def test_viewing_booked_when_confirmation_and_time_in_different_messages():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Confirmed, I'll see you then", "sender": "landlord"},
        {"message": "Saturday at 2pm", "sender": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

    # Reverse order also counts
    messages = [
        {"message": "Saturday at 2pm", "sender": "tenant"},
        {"message": "Confirmed, I'll see you then", "sender": "landlord"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED


def test_fallback_keys_message_and_sender():
    # Use "content" instead of "message" and "direction" instead of "sender"
    messages = [
        {"content": "Confirmed, see you Thursday at 14:30", "direction": "landlord"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

    messages = [
        {"content": "I think we need to reschedule", "direction": "tenant"},
    ]
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION


def test_viewing_discussion_when_scheduling_discussed_without_confirmation():
    messages = [
        {"message": "Are you available next Tuesday?", "sender": "tenant"},
        {"message": "What time works for you?", "sender": "landlord"},
    ]
    # No confirmation or time, but scheduling discussed, so VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_extract_time_only_defaults_to_today_or_tomorrow():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 16, 0)  # 4pm
    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet at 3pm"}]  # 3pm today is before now, so roll forward one day
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 11, 15, 0)
    assert result == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet at 5pm"}]  # 5pm today is after now, so same day
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 17, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_with_tomorrow_keyword():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Confirmed, meet at 4pm tomorrow"}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 11, 16, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_with_fallback_content_key():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"content": "Meeting at 14:30"}]  # no "message" key, fallback to "content"
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 14, 30)
    assert result == expected

def test_ignore_numeric_calendar_dates_not_as_time():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet on 12/06"}]  # date only, no time
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result is None

def test_bare_hours_1_to_7_assumed_pm():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 8, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 3"}]  # bare hour 3 assumed pm = 15:00
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 15, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_returns_none_when_no_time_mentioned():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "No time mentioned here"}, {"content": "Just some text"}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result is None

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_extract_time_only_message_defaults_to_today_or_tomorrow():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 16, 0)  # 4pm
    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet at 3pm"}]
    # 3pm today is before now (4pm), so should roll forward to tomorrow 3pm
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 11, 15, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_with_tomorrow_keyword():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0)  # 10am
    messages = [{"message": "Confirmed, meet at 4pm tomorrow"}]
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 11, 16, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_with_numeric_calendar_date_ignored_as_time():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Meeting on 12/06 at 14:30"}]
    # The date 12/06 is a calendar date, so time 14:30 should be extracted with that date
    # But spec says digits part of numeric calendar date must not be misread as time of day
    # So time 14:30 should be extracted, date from message is 12/06 (assumed June 12)
    # However, spec is ambiguous about parsing calendar date, so we test that time is extracted with today or tomorrow logic
    # Since calendar date parsing is not specified, assume time is extracted with today/tomorrow logic ignoring calendar date
    # So time 14:30 today (June 10) at 14:30 is after now 10:00, so date is today
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 14, 30)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_with_fallback_content_key():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"content": "Meeting at 4 pm"}]
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 16, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_with_bare_hours_assumed_pm():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 8, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet at 5"}]
    # Bare hour 5 assumed pm -> 17:00 today, which is after now 8:00
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 17, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_return_none_when_no_time_mentioned():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "No time mentioned here"}, {"content": "Just chatting"}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result is None

import pytest
from app.ai.personas import get_conversation_style

def test_canonical_style_returns_correct_config():
    config = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    assert isinstance(config, dict)
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in config
    assert config["phone_fetching_type"] == "viewing_first"

def test_legacy_aliases_resolve_to_canonical_config():
    aliases = {
        "viewing_first": "friendly_viewing",
        "friendly_couple": "warm_casual",
        "direct_professional": "direct_number_request",
        "relocation_approach": "video_call_request",
        "whatsapp_first": "whatsapp_coordination",
    }
    expected_phone_fetching = {
        "friendly_viewing": "viewing_first",
        "warm_casual": "delayed",
        "direct_number_request": "immediate",
        "video_call_request": "immediate",
        "whatsapp_coordination": "whatsapp_first",
    }
    for alias, canonical in aliases.items():
        config = get_conversation_style(alias)
        assert isinstance(config, dict)
        assert "phone_fetching_type" in config
        assert config["phone_fetching_type"] == expected_phone_fetching[canonical]

def test_unknown_style_falls_back_to_friendly_viewing():
    config = get_conversation_style("non_existent_style")
    assert isinstance(config, dict)
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in config
    assert config["phone_fetching_type"] == "viewing_first"

def test_empty_string_style_falls_back_to_friendly_viewing():
    config = get_conversation_style("")
    assert isinstance(config, dict)
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in config
    assert config["phone_fetching_type"] == "viewing_first"

def test_friendly_couple_alias_phone_fetching_type_is_delayed_and_no_exception():
    # This tests the explicit example in the spec
    config = get_conversation_style("friendly_couple")
    assert isinstance(config, dict)
    assert "phone_fetching_type" in config
    assert config["phone_fetching_type"] == "delayed"

from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count
import pytest

def test_none_and_empty_input():
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0

def test_count_using_sender_key_case_insensitive():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User"},
        {"sender": "TENANT"},
        {"sender": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "Ai"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "other"},
    ]
    # Only first four count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_count_using_direction_key_fallback_case_insensitive():
    messages = [
        {"direction": "user"},
        {"direction": "TENANT"},
        {"direction": "Outbound"},
        {"direction": "AI"},
        {"direction": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"direction": "other"},
    ]
    # Only first four count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4

def test_sender_key_takes_precedence_over_direction():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "direction": "user"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "direction": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "inbound", "direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "ai", "direction": "inbound"},
    ]
    # sender keys: landlord (no), tenant (yes), inbound (no), ai (yes)
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 2

def test_mixed_senders_and_directions():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user"},
        {"direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "outbound"},
        {"direction": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "ai"},
        {"direction": "user"},
    ]
    # Count: sender keys user, outbound, ai (3), direction keys tenant, user (2)
    # total 5
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 5

def test_messages_with_unrelated_keys_and_values():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user"},
        {"direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "unknown"},
        {"direction": "unknown"},
        {"foo": "bar"},
        {},
    ]
    # Only first two count
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 2

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_extract_time_only_defaults_to_today_or_tomorrow():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 16, 0)  # 4pm
    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet at 3pm"}]
    # 3pm today is before now 4pm, so rolls forward to tomorrow
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 11, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet at 5pm"}]
    # 5pm today is after now 4pm, so same day
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 17, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

def test_extract_time_with_tomorrow_keyword():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 10, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Confirmed, meet at 4pm tomorrow"}]
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 11, 16, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

def test_extract_time_with_24h_and_pm_am_rules():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 14:30"}]
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 14, 30)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 3pm"}]
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 4 pm"}]
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 16, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 1"}]
    # bare hour 1 assumed pm
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 13, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 7"}]
    # bare hour 7 assumed pm
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 19, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

def test_digits_in_calendar_date_not_misread_as_time():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Meeting on 12/06 at 3pm"}]
    # 3pm is valid time, date is 12/06 (12 June)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 12, 15, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

    messages = [{"message": "Meeting on 12/06"}]
    # no time, only date, so returns None
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) is None

def test_fallback_to_content_key_and_limit_to_most_recent_8_messages():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    # 9 messages, only last 8 considered
    messages = [{"message": "Meeting at 3pm"}] + [{"message": "No time here"}]*7 + [{"content": "Meeting at 5pm"}]
    # The first message with 3pm is ignored because it's the 9th oldest, only last 8 considered
    # The last message with 5pm is considered
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 17, 0)
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) == expected

def test_returns_none_when_no_time_mentioned():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 10, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"message": "Let's meet soon"}, {"content": "No time here either"}]
    assert extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now) is None

from app.ai.conversation_memory import landlord_messages

def test_none_input_returns_empty_list():
    assert landlord_messages(None) == []

def test_empty_list_input_returns_empty_list():
    assert landlord_messages([]) == []

def test_messages_with_sender_landlord_or_inbound_case_insensitive():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "LandLord", "message": "Hi again"},
        {"sender": "INBOUND", "message": "Welcome"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Not landlord"},
        {"sender": "outbound", "message": "Not landlord either"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "LandLord", "message": "Hi again"},
        {"sender": "INBOUND", "message": "Welcome"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_messages_with_no_sender_fallback_to_direction():
    messages = [
        {"direction": "inbound", "message": "Fallback landlord"},
        {"direction": "outbound", "message": "Not landlord"},
        {"message": "No sender or direction"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"direction": "inbound", "message": "Fallback landlord"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_order_preserved_and_original_dicts_returned():
    m1 = {"sender": "landlord", "message": "First"}
    m2 = {"direction": "inbound", "message": "Second"}
    m3 = {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Third"}
    messages = [m1, m2, m3]
    result = landlord_messages(messages)
    assert result == [m1, m2]
    assert result[0] is m1
    assert result[1] is m2

def test_messages_with_sender_direction_case_insensitive_and_partial_words():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Exact match"},
        {"sender": "inbound", "message": "Exact match"},
        {"sender": "inbounded", "message": "Should not match"},
        {"direction": "INBOUND", "message": "Fallback exact match"},
        {"direction": "outbound", "message": "Fallback no match"},
        {"sender": "land", "message": "Partial no match"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Exact match"},
        {"sender": "inbound", "message": "Exact match"},
        {"direction": "INBOUND", "message": "Fallback exact match"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected
